_comment: Speaker-faithful catalyst config. Copy to catalysts.json and update manually.

mega_ipos.name: SpaceX
mega_ipos.notes: The speaker treats SpaceX as the ultimate liquidity litmus test — if this cannot get done cleanly at scale, it signals the market is not as deep as the tape suggests.

mega_ipos.name: OpenAI
mega_ipos.notes: AI thematic demand test. Valuation reception will signal how much premium the market still puts on AI narrative.

mega_ipos.name: Anthropic
mega_ipos.notes: Secondary AI thematic test alongside OpenAI.

fed_chair.name: Kevin Hassett
fed_chair.notes: More politically aligned with aggressive cut path. Speaker interprets as equity-supportive and faster-easing bias.

fed_chair.name: Chris Waller
fed_chair.notes: Data-dependent. More cautious on rushing cuts before inflation is clearly beaten.

fed_chair.name: Kevin Warsh
fed_chair.notes: Historically credibility-focused. Slower, more careful easing path.

tariffs.notes: Track tariff escalation as an inflation pass-through risk. If tariffs broaden and escalate, they can keep CPI stickier even as growth weakens — a direct stagflation complication.

plumbing.notes: The speaker distinguishes between genuine QE and emergency liquidity plumbing. Emergency operations can signal stress rather than bullish expansion.
